criteria_results.title: Allegation 1: Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations â€” Identified
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output includes a dedicated section titled 'Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations' that clearly identifies this as a distinct allegation category. It describes the pattern of disproportionately allocating profitable trades to Oceanic Fund I LP and Chao's personal account, references Sections 206(1) and 206(2) of the Advisers Act, and provides specific details about the statistical disparity (27.2 percentage point difference in profitable allocation rates) and financial harm ($7,800,000 to Oceanic Fund I LP and $438,000 to Chao's personal account).

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: Correct Statutory Provisions Cited
criteria_results.reasoning: The Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations section explicitly states: 'The Division alleges that during the Review Period, WCA, Whitmore, and Chao engaged in a pattern of favorable trade allocation commonly referred to as "cherry-picking," in violation of Sections 206(1) and 206(2) of the Advisers Act.' Both statutory provisions are correctly cited in connection with the cherry-picking allegation.

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: WCA Identified as Implicated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly identifies WCA (Whitmore Capital Advisors LLC) as an implicated party in the cherry-picking allegation. Whitmore holds a 12% ownership stake.' The 'Dual Role Responsibility' section also names Whitmore as implicated across all six allegation categories.

Chao's personal brokerage account (Account No. ending -7823)'.

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: Key Quantitative Facts â€” Trade Counts and Rates
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output includes only 2 of the 5 required quantitative facts: (3) Oceanic Fund I LP profitable allocation rate of 68.4% and (4) other client accounts rate of 41.2% (mentioned in the Directed Brokerage Scheme and Conflict of Interest sections), and (5) t-statistic of 4.73 (p < 0.001) (mentioned in the Conflict of Interest section). That's actually 3 facts if we count the t-statistic. Let me recheck: (3) 68.4% - YES (Directed Brokerage Scheme: 'allocated rate of 68.4% compared to 41.2% for others'); (4) 41.2% - YES; (5) t-statistic of 4.73 (p < 0.001) - YES (Conflict of Interest section). However, (1) 4,217 equity trades analyzed and (2) 1,843 profitable same-day trades are NOT present in the output. The output does mention 5,776 omnibus equity trades but not 4,217 analyzed trades or 1,843 profitable same-day trades. So the output contains exactly 3 of the 5 required facts (68.4%, 41.2%, and t-statistic of 4.73), which meets the threshold of at least 3. Therefore this should PASS.
